description:
Advances: specled.severity, specled.spec_drift_trailer, specled.policy_files, specled.prose_guard

Deliverable:
Severity resolver with precedence off > trailer > config > per-code default; branch_check/trailer.ex scanning base..HEAD; PolicyFiles classifier with test/lib/doc/generated/root_meta/other classification (priv/ = :lib, priv/plts/ = :generated); ChangeAnalysis delegates to PolicyFiles; Config.BranchGuard + Config.Prose zoi schemas; prose-rot finding (spec_requirement_too_short) emitted from mix spec.validate.

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/branch_check/trailer_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/policy_files_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/config/prose_test.exs
  - mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_tasks_test.exs (prose-rot finding)
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.severity.scenario.off_beats_trailer
  - specled.spec_drift_trailer.scenario.refactor_downgrades_realization_drift
  - specled.policy_files.scenario.priv_repo_migrations_is_lib
  - specled.policy_files.scenario.priv_plts_is_generated
  - specled.prose_guard.scenario.too_short_must_flagged

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check/**
- lib/specled_ex/policy_files.ex
- lib/specled_ex/change_analysis.ex
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex
- lib/specled_ex/config/branch_guard.ex
- lib/specled_ex/config/prose.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.validate.ex
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/**
- test/specled_ex/policy_files_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/config/**
- CHANGELOG.md
- .spec/AGENTS.md

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/schema/**
- lib/specled_ex/realization/**
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/**
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/**
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.cover.test.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.suggest_binding.ex
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.triangle.ex
- mix.exs (do not add stream_data here; that lands in S2)
- test/fixtures/sample_project/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- No realized_by schema work — S2 owns it.
- No new external deps in mix.exs.
- No changes to HashStore, Binding resolution, or any tier orchestrator.
- Do not inline Severity precedence at call sites — every finding/4 call must route through Severity.resolve/3.
- Do not add per-requirement prose opt-outs (scope cut #7); only global severity override via config.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.check passes on specled_ex's own repo
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Summary: The severity resolver centralizes finding-severity policy so
every finding routes through one place (trailer > config > default, with
:off absorbing trailers). The Spec-Drift trailer parser scans base..HEAD
so squash/merge-commit PRs preserve author intent. PolicyFiles replaces
scattered prefix checks in ChangeAnalysis with a total classifier (:lib
:test :doc :generated :unknown); priv/ defaults to :lib, only priv/plts/
carves to :generated. Config.BranchGuard + Config.Prose add zoi-backed
workspace-level overrides. The prose-rot finding emits
spec_requirement_too_short from mix spec.validate for must requirements
under the char/word threshold and is silenceable via :off.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: 71f1c51a-63fb-4092-a147-99a48bff7714
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/71f1c51a-63fb-4092-a147-99a48bff7714.jsonl

Reasoning: The VERIFY comment's two findings both reproduce under independent check. (1) Severity-routing intent violation is real: lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex does not alias or call SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.Severity at all; its four finding/4 call sites at branch_check.ex:21,40,57,134 use hardcoded "error"/"warning" strings, and severity_from_config/1 at 149-150 re-implements precedence inline for test_tags. The resolver is only consumed from lib/specled_ex/config/prose.ex:121. The ticket's Out-of-Scope (intent) line — 'every finding/4 call must route through Severity.resolve/3' — is the one risk the ticket flagged loudest, and it materialized. (2) specled.policy_files.change_analysis_delegates has zero test coverage: grep for 'ChangeAnalysis' across test/ returns no hits, so no test would fail if someone re-inlined prefix checks in change_analysis.ex. Other requirements are well-covered, mechanical scope is clean, no out-of-scope (files) touches, no new deps, no per-requirement prose opt-outs, no realized_by/HashStore/Binding/tier work.

If BOUNCE, specify for whom:
- BOUNCE → implementer: Refactor lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex to route every finding through Severity.resolve/3. Concretely: add alias SpecLedEx.BranchCheck.Severity; pass config_severities (and, when feasible, trailer_override via Trailer.read/2) into run/3; replace the four hardcoded severity strings at :21/:40/:57/:134 with Severity.resolve(code, opts, per_code_default); delete severity_from_config/1 and route the test_tags decision through the resolver with an appropriate per-code default. Add a test in test/specled_ex/branch_check/ that would fail if a finding were emitted with a severity not produced by Severity.resolve/3 (e.g., assert that config severities { branch_guard_unmapped_change: off } suppresses the finding end-to-end through BranchCheck.run). Also add one test that references SpecLedEx.ChangeAnalysis.classify/1 or co_change_rule/1 directly — asserting delegation to PolicyFiles — so specled.policy_files.change_analysis_delegates is actually guarded.
- BOUNCE → verifier: none; verifier correctly identified both gaps.

IMPLEMENTED (iteration 2)

Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/71f1c51a-63fb-4092-a147-99a48bff7714.jsonl

Commit: fb04778 S1: route BranchCheck findings through Severity.resolve/3

Responds to both gaps identified by AUDIT: BOUNCE → implementer.

Gap 1 (Severity-routing intent violation) — fixed:
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex now aliases Severity + Trailer + Config and
  loads a severity_opts keyword (config_severities + trailer_override) at
  the start of run/3.
- A single emit/6 helper replaces every finding/4 call site. It resolves
  severity via Severity.resolve/3 with a per-code default (:error for the
  three branch_guard_* codes, :warning for branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag)
  and drops findings whose resolved severity is :off.
- severity_from_config/1 is gone. test_tags.enforcement still works — it
  flows in as a per-code default override so the historical contract is
  preserved, but config.severities / Spec-Drift trailers win via the
  Severity.resolve/3 precedence chain.

Gap 2 (change_analysis_delegates has no failing-if-violated test) — fixed:
- test/specled_ex/policy_files_test.exs gained a 'ChangeAnalysis delegation'
  describe block with four tests:
  (a) ChangeAnalysis.classify/1 agrees with PolicyFiles on 14 representative paths
  (b) ChangeAnalysis.co_change_rule/1 agrees with PolicyFiles on paths and kinds
  (c) ChangeAnalysis.policy_target?/1 agrees with PolicyFiles on representative paths
  (d) source-level defense: refute @policy_prefixes, @policy_root_files,
      String.starts_with?(path, \"lib/\"), or priv/ prefix checks inside
      ChangeAnalysis — re-inlining the delegation would fail this test.

Additional coverage added:
- test/specled_ex/branch_check/severity_integration_test.exs, four tests:
  1. config.severities :off suppresses branch_guard_unmapped_change in a real
     BranchCheck.run/3 call.
  2. config.severities can escalate branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag
     to :error (previously this code was 'warning' hardcoded).
  3. Source-level defense: alias to Severity present, Severity.resolve( called,
     no severity_from_config defp, no hardcoded 'error'/'warning' strings on
     branch_guard codes.
  4. config.severities :off suppresses branch_guard_missing_decision_update
     on a cross-cutting change.

Tests: 213 pass, 0 failures (was 205; added 8).
Compile: clean with --warnings-as-errors.
mix spec.check: status still pre-existing — 54 surface_target_missing
warnings on S2-S5 specs; zero warnings on subjects this ticket advances.

Notes: Both AUDIT: BOUNCE gaps are closed with the right depth of fix.
1. Severity routing intent: branch_check.ex now has one emit/6 helper, which is the sole egress for findings and routes every emission through Severity.resolve/3. The integration test at severity_integration_test.exs:91-111 is a source-level guard that would fail if a regressor re-hardcoded severities. title: S1: cheap escape hatches — severity, Spec-Drift trailer, PolicyFiles, prose-rot
